commit.message:
fix: recording produces 1KB empty files — H264Auto uses codec::Id not NVENC

Root cause: H264Auto was trying h264_nvenc first which opens without error
but fails silently at send_frame time when CUDA is unavailable.

Fix: H264Auto now calls encoder::find(codec::Id::H264) — identical to the
original code before codec-pref was added. The system's default H264 encoder
is used (libx264 on most systems, NVENC on systems where it's registered
as default).

New VideoCodecPref design:
- H264Auto: encoder::find(H264) — original behavior, always works
- H265Auto: encoder::find(HEVC) — H265 via system default encoder
- H264Nvenc: explicit h264_nvenc (GPU only, no fallback)
- H265Nvenc: explicit hevc_nvenc (GPU only, no fallback)

NVENC is now opt-in (users who know they have a working GPU select it).
Auto modes are guaranteed to work the same as before.

If the system default H264 encoder happens to be NVENC, H264Auto passes
NVENC-compatible options automatically (detected via name.contains('nvenc')).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(media,text): unify module structure

Restructure media and text modules to match zsys standard layout:

  module/
  ├── __init__.py          # Re-exports from bindings
  ├── c/                   # Native C implementation
  │   ├── *.h, *.c
  │   └── CMakeLists.txt
  └── bindings/
      ├── python/          # Python bindings
      │   ├── __init__.py
      │   └── submodules/
      ├── go/
      ├── rust/
      └── ...

This matches telegram/, utils/, and other zsys modules.
Import paths unchanged for end users:
  from zsys.media import image, video
  from zsys.text import html, parse

  - image.c: media_image_resize with libswscale

feat(telegram): add Pyrogram-compat methods to Message + re-export modules API

Message class now has convenience methods matching Pyrogram API:
  - edit(), edit_text() - edit message text
  - delete() - delete message
  - reply(), reply_text() - reply to message
  - forward() - forward to another chat
  - copy() - copy without forward header
  - pin(), unpin() - pin/unpin message
  - download() - download media
  - get_reply_message(), get_reply() - fetch replied-to message
  - command property - parse /command args

Chat and _MessageChat classes now have:
  - linked_chat property - get linked chat object
  - first_name, last_name properties - Pyrogram compat
  - photo property placeholder
  - permissions, linked_chat_id fields

pyrogram/__init__.py now re-exports module management functions:

This enables existing Pyrogram-style userbot code to work with TDLib.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(modules): infer platform from ctx type annotation

If the handler is typed with a concrete Context subclass, the platform
is now inferred automatically — no need to write platform= explicitly:

    # before
    @command("forward", platform="tdlib")
    async def forward_cmd(ctx: Context): ...

    # after — type hint is enough
    @command("forward")
    async def forward_cmd(ctx: TelegramContext): ...

Explicit platform= still works and overrides the inferred value.
Base Context (or no annotation) → any platform (unchanged).

Implementation: _infer_platform(func) reads typing.get_type_hints(),
checks the first parameter, and returns the platform class variable
of any Context subclass. Called in decorator() before building Command.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(modules): add platform= filter to @command decorator

Adds optional platform restriction to Command and the @command() decorator.

  @command('forward', platform='tdlib')        # Telegram only
  @command('ping',    platform=['tdlib', 'discord'])  # multiple
  @command('help')                              # any (unchanged)

When platform is set, the wrapper silently returns None if ctx.platform
does not match — no change needed inside handler code.  Handlers that
need platform-specific APIs can still use if ctx.platform == ... for
fine-grained branching.

Changes:
- Command.platform: str | list[str] | None = None
- Router.command() + global command(): new platform= kwarg
- wrapper: checks ctx.platform against allowed list before calling handler

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  callback via ConcurrentHashMap<Long, handler>

  TelegramClient (AutoCloseable), Java-style Consumer<TelegramContext>
  handlers

fix(arch): Pyrogram-style clean API — 4 architectural fixes

   — msg.edit() / msg.delete() / msg.reply() were broken on history msgs
   because _client was None (Message(lib, ptr) without self)

2. FloodWait.value alias: Pyrogram uses .value, zsys had only .seconds
   — both now available: e.seconds == e.value

   message is a reply; reply_to_id is always set

4. TdlibContext.reply/edit/delete: use raw.chat_id directly
   — was going through _MessageChat shim (self.chat.id); now

commit.message:
feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and
auto-implement steps with a recursive fs.readdirSync('lua') call.
This keeps codebase context up to date automatically as new Lua
modules are added without requiring workflow changes.

Previously only impact < 4 triggered the label. Now type=other always
gets 'evaluation needed' regardless of impact score, matching the
intended behaviour that unclassified issues always need manual review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

commit.message:
Fix rendering efficiency and screencopy Y-flip

Rendering: Skip the full bind/clear/draw/finish/submit cycle when
nothing visual has changed (no new Electron frames, no dirty Wayland
surfaces, no cursor movement). In the DRM path, also skip when no
screencopy or portal streams are active. Frame callbacks are always
sent unconditionally to prevent Wayland client stalls.

Screencopy: Remove the incorrect row-flip after glReadPixels in both
the wlr-screencopy fulfillment and portal stream readback paths.
Smithay already applies a Y-correction when rendering to GBM-backed
FBOs, so glReadPixels returns top-down data. The manual flip was
inverting an already-correct image, producing upside-down output.
